skill.name: mobile-testing
skill.description: Create, run, and troubleshoot React Native tests with Jest and React Native Testing Library.
skill.summary: Guides React Native component, hook, utility, form, list, and async testing workflows.
skill.author: BabakBar

security_audit.summary: The static command findings are mostly fenced documentation examples for npm, Jest, and React Native Testing Library. No prompt injection, credential access, data exfiltration, or malicious intent was found in SKILL.md, but users should review project package scripts before running npm commands.

medium_findings.title: Bash Test Commands Require User Review
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md includes Bash examples for installing test dependencies and running npm test scripts. This is appropriate for a mobile testing skill, but npm scripts can execute project-defined commands, so users should inspect package.json before running them.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible in fenced Bash documentation and match the stated testing purpose. Residual risk remains because npm install and npm test can invoke package lifecycle or project scripts.

low_findings.title: Documentation Links Are Static URLs
low_findings.description: SKILL.md links to official Jest and React Native Testing Library documentation. These URLs are not evidence of data exfiltration or unauthorized network activity.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The detected URLs are ordinary documentation links at the end of the guide. They do not collect data, embed tracking code, or instruct outbound requests with secrets.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Hits Dismissed
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography locations are React Native testing examples and metadata text. No hashing, encryption, cipher selection, or credential handling code was found at these locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines shows descriptions and Jest describe blocks, not cryptographic operations. The static matches are false positives.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Static Hits Dismissed
low_findings.description: The reported reconnaissance locations are test examples for invalid input handling and rendered text assertions. No host inspection, environment enumeration, or system discovery commands were found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are normal Jest assertions in sample tests. They do not reference operating system data, network configuration, users, files, or environment variables.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill instructs users to run npm install, npm test, npm run test:watch, and npm run test:coverage. These are legitimate test commands but should be run only in trusted projects.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The Bash commands are explicit and directly related to the skill purpose. The concern is contextual execution risk, not confirmed malicious behavior.

content.value_statement: React Native teams need fast, reliable tests for components, hooks, utilities, and app flows. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical testing patterns using Jest and React Native Testing Library.
actual_capabilities: Creates component tests with render and fireEvent patterns.
Shows hook tests with renderHook and act.
Provides utility test examples for valid and invalid inputs.
Documents mocks for Expo notifications, database clients, and navigation.
Lists npm commands for test runs, watch mode, coverage, specific files, and snapshots.
Gives troubleshooting guidance for timeouts, missing elements, mocks, and async tests.
limitations: Does not include Detox, Appium, or other end-to-end mobile testing tools.
Does not verify that a project already has compatible Jest configuration.
Does not provide platform-specific iOS or Android simulator setup steps.
Example commands may need changes for non-npm package managers.

use_cases.title: Add Component Tests
use_cases.description: Create tests that verify visible output and press behavior for React Native UI components.

use_cases.title: Standardize Test Patterns
use_cases.description: Adopt consistent templates for hooks, utilities, forms, lists, mocks, and async UI states.

use_cases.title: Debug Failing Tests
use_cases.description: Use practical troubleshooting guidance for timing failures, missing elements, mock order, and async assertions.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Component Test
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the mobile-testing skill to write a React Native Testing Library test for this component. Cover rendering and one user interaction.

prompt_templates.title: Test a Custom Hook
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the mobile-testing skill to create Jest tests for this React Native hook. Include state changes and edge cases.

prompt_templates.title: Mock App Dependencies
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the mobile-testing skill to test this feature while mocking navigation, database access, and external modules.

prompt_templates.title: Improve a Test Suite
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the mobile-testing skill to review these failing React Native tests, identify fragile selectors, and suggest stronger coverage.

output_examples.input: Test a button component with a title and press handler.
output_examples.output: A focused component test plan that checks rendered text, triggers the press action, and verifies the handler call.

output_examples.input: Create tests for a counter hook.
output_examples.output: A hook testing approach that renders the hook, performs state updates inside act, and verifies the resulting count.

output_examples.input: Debug a test that cannot find an element.
output_examples.output: A troubleshooting checklist covering selector choice, rendered output inspection, async queries, and mock placement.

best_practices: Inspect package.json before running npm scripts from any project.
Prefer behavior-focused assertions over implementation details.
Mock external dependencies to keep tests deterministic and fast.
anti_patterns: Do not run npm install in an untrusted repository without review.
Do not rely only on snapshots for behavior that users can perform.
Do not test private implementation details when visible behavior is available.

faq.question: What test framework does this skill use?
faq.answer: It focuses on Jest and React Native Testing Library for React Native unit and integration tests.

faq.question: Can it help with hooks?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a renderHook and act pattern for testing custom hook state changes.

faq.question: Does it cover end-to-end testing?
faq.answer: No. It does not cover Detox, Appium, device farms, or simulator automation.

faq.question: Can it help mock Expo modules?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes examples for mocking Expo notifications, Expo Router navigation, and database clients.

faq.question: Is it safe to run the listed commands?
faq.answer: The commands are standard testing commands, but review package.json before running npm scripts in a project.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
